description: SAML Signature Wrapping (XSW1-8) and signature-exclusion attacks. Detects Identity-Provider responses parsed without strict signature-coverage validation. Targets: SAMLResponse POST endpoints, ACS URLs. References: SAML Raider, OWASP SAML Security Cheat Sheet.

saml_response_endpoint_detect.description: ACS (Assertion Consumer Service) endpoints — POST body has SAMLResponse base64 parameter; or query SAMLResponse=. Confirms target consumes SAML.

xsw_signature_wrap.description: XSW1/2: wrap original signed Assertion inside attacker-injected Assertion. If processor reads attacker's Assertion but validates original Assertion's signature, auth bypass.

probes.description: Wrapped-signature attack — operator builds the actual payload via SAML Raider / python3 -c with original captured response. Server logs admin login = vuln.

xsw_comment_injection_nameid.description: Comment-injection in NameID. Parser canonicalises before signature check; canonicalisation strips XML comments, so 'admin<!---->@evil.com' validates as 'admin' but app reads 'admin@evil.com'.

probes.description: Inject HTML comment in NameID — operator builds via raider

saml_signature_exclusion.description: Send unsigned Assertion (no ds:Signature). Vulnerable processors accept assertions without verifying signatures were present.

probes.description: Signature-exclusion — assertion contains no ds:Signature element. Operator builds.

saml_keyinfo_swap.description: KeyInfo manipulation — replace IdP cert in <ds:KeyInfo> with attacker key, re-sign whole assertion. Vulnerable verifier trusts embedded KeyInfo rather than out-of-band cert.

probes.payload: BASE64(...full attacker-signed assertion with attacker KeyInfo X509Certificate...)
probes.description: Operator builds with python3 + lxml + xmlsec
